actionCalls.description: Calls the AgentSOQLQuery Apex action to execute the provided SOQL query string and return results as JSON.
actionCalls.name: Execute_Query
actionCalls.label: Execute SOQL Query

Flow.interviewLabel: WSM - ALF - Agent: Execute SOQL Query {!$Flow.CurrentDateTime}
Flow.label: WSM - ALF - Agent: Execute SOQL Query

variables.description: The SOQL query string to execute. Write standard SOQL syntax: SELECT fields FROM Object WHERE conditions. Always include a LIMIT clause. Example: SELECT Id, Name FROM Account WHERE Industry = 'Technology' LIMIT 10. The query runs in the current user's security context so only accessible records and fields are returned.
variables.name: query

variables.description: Maximum number of records to return. Defaults to 200 if not provided. Maximum allowed value is 2000. If the query already contains a LIMIT clause, this value is ignored. Use lower values when you only need a few results.
variables.name: maxRecords

variables.description: JSON array of the query results. Each element is an object with field API names as keys and their values. Returns an empty array [] if no records match the query. Parse this JSON to extract the specific values you need.
variables.name: resultsJson

variables.description: The number of records returned by the query. Use this to check if any results were found before attempting to parse the results JSON.
variables.name: recordCount

variables.description: True if the query executed successfully, false if there was an error. Always check this before using the results. When false, read errorMessage for details.
variables.name: success

variables.description: Description of the error when success is false. Common errors include: invalid field name, invalid object name, SOQL syntax error, or insufficient access permissions. Empty when success is true.
variables.name: errorMessage
